what_it_does: Records metadata for an image attachment in the design-assets channel, binding an attachment id to an image target and logging when it was added.
observations: The file consists only of YAML frontmatter with no body content
standards: Uses an image:// URI scheme for the target
Includes a parenthetical qualifier (30) in the target path
Serves as a channel-specific attachment ledger entry
conventions: image:// URI for referencing image assets
concerns: Target string contains spaces and parentheses that may need escaping or normalization
No human-readable title, filename, or description for the attachment
No author or modification history recorded
notes: A minimal ledger entry that registers an image attachment in the design-assets channel and links it to a specific image target.
questions: What does the (30) qualifier in the target represent?
Is image:// a resolvable protocol or a project-specific identifier?
Is this file generated automatically or authored by a user?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the content: it is an attachment metadata record for an image in the design-assets channel.
intention_signals: To create a durable, queryable record that associates a channel attachment identifier with a specific image asset and captures the moment it was added.
novelty: The target field uses an image:// URI with a parenthetical numeric qualifier, which is a project-specific referencing convention.
contribution: Provides a concrete binding between an attachment id and an image asset target within the design-assets channel, including a creation timestamp, which a fused system would keep as a reference entry.
summary_for_embedding: A channel-memory attachment ledger file for the design-assets channel that registers image attachment att-a989d7b7 targeting image://design-assets/icons/image (30) with an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To record the existence and target of an image attachment added to the design-assets channel.
